'''Orleon''' was a rainy [[Planet/Legends|planet]] in the [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim]]. The rain was torrential and never stopped, leaving the planet covered in mud.

==History==
In [[20 BBY/Legends|19.9 BBY]], the [[Freighter/Legends|freighter]] ''[[Spinner]]'' was believed to have contracted [[Stone mite/Legends|stone mites]] on Orleon before crashing at [[Westport]], [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]]. As he believed this indicated a [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]] facility on the planet, [[Senate Bureau of Intelligence/Legends|Senate Bureau of Intelligence]] [[Director of the Senate Bureau of Intelligence|Director]] [[Armand Isard/Legends|Armand Isard]] ordered the planet infected with weapons-adapted stone mites. They ate through the planet's crust, and Orleon's entire population of 800 million was forced to evacuate.<ref name="TNEC">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''</ref>

These creatures could eat through anything, even solid rock and military-grade [[Starship/Legends|starship]] hull plating with the natural acids they produced. They reproduced rapidly, and usually moved in swarms thousands to millions of stone mites strong, eating everything in their way. This made Orleon a hazardous place for [[Spacer/Legends|spacers]] to land.<ref name="TNEC" />

==Behind the scenes==
In [[Star Wars (1977) 28|''Marvel Star Wars 28'']], [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] rescues "[[Jabba Desilijic Tiure/Legends|Jabba the Hut]]" ''(sic)'' from stone mites on Orleon, leading the crimelord to briefly cancel Solo's debt. However, Jabba was at this time depicted as a [[Nimbanel/Legends|Nimbanel]], based on a background character from [[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|''A New Hope'']] later identified as Jabba's accountant [[Mosep Binneed/Legends|Mosep Binneed]].

Jabba's Databank entry states that "While this particular version of these events didn't happen, it is still possible that the Orleon incident did occur."<ref name="DB-Jabba the Hutt">{{DB|character|jabbathehutt|Jabba the Hutt}}</ref> Many fans have speculated that Han rescued Mosep Binneed rather than Jabba, and further speculated that Mosep used the alias "Jabba the Hut".

The Marvel comic places the planet in the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]]; however, ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' places it in the Mid Rim.
